title: QUIC RFC 9000 NEW_TOKEN ACK Piggyback Topoff Verification
verification_method: Requirement-home execution, ACK piggyback policy benchmark dry guard, SpecTrace render/check, attempted JSON validation, and diff hygiene for the bounded explicit-path 1-RTT NEW_TOKEN ACK piggyback topoff.
scope: This verification covers server NEW_TOKEN packets emitted through the explicit-path 1-RTT application send helper after client address validation when Application Data ACK-generation state may already contain pending ack-eliciting receipts.
expected_result: Focused tests prove that pending Application Data ACK state is prepended to a server NEW_TOKEN packet emitted after path validation, the NEW_TOKEN frame remains present after the ACK frame, no ACK frame is invented when no ACK is pending, and the sent ACK frame suppresses duplicate ACK-only triggers for the covered packet receipt. The benchmark dry guard validates the reused ACK piggyback scheduling suite without making public or MSQUIC comparison claims.
procedure: Run the focused RFC 9000 requirement-home tests for S13P2P1-0010, S9P3-0005, and S9P3-0011.
Run the ACK piggyback policy benchmark suite with the BenchmarkDotNet Dry job and preserve logs and reports.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
Run canonical SpecTrace JSON validation and preserve the output, including standing repository-wide residual failures if present.
preconditions: The runtime is in the server role and has 1-RTT Application Data packet protection material.
A new client address has been validated so the server-only NEW_TOKEN emission path is eligible to send.
ACK-generation state has already recorded pending Application Data packet receipts before the NEW_TOKEN packet is composed.
Initial, Handshake, general candidate-path scheduling, endpoint interop, public API, and general delayed-ACK timer behavior are outside this verification.

supplemental_sections.heading: Benchmark Boundary
supplemental_sections.content: The benchmark evidence is the existing internal Incursa-only ACK piggyback policy suite reused by this explicit-path topoff. It is not a public facade, System.Net.Quic, MSQUIC, HTTP/3, or interop comparison.

status_summary: Focused runtime proof passed locally on 2026-04-25 for the bounded explicit-path 1-RTT NEW_TOKEN ACK piggyback topoff. Repository-wide SpecTrace JSON validation may still report standing residual Markdown and unresolved-reference backlog; the failure log is preserved as evidence rather than reclassified as a focused behavior failure.
